frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis of fMRI
frameworks.one_line: decoding stimulus content from hemodynamic activity patterns during WM delay

frameworks.name: sensory-recruitment of working memory
frameworks.one_line: WM content represented in sensory cortex extended here to auditory features

frameworks.name: feature-selective WM representation
frameworks.one_line: two concurrent features of one object can each be decoded separately

core_question: Can two separate features (pitch and perceived location) of a single auditory object be concurrently decoded from delay-period brain activity?
key_assumptions: stimulus-specific WM content elicits feature-selective patterns decodable by MVPA
pitch and location are dissociable features of a single sound
manipulations: feature to be reproduced at recall (pitch vs location)
memorized sound's pitch and perceived location
selection of one of two sample sounds for maintenance
key_findings: both pitch and location showed comparable discriminability at recall
feature-selective delay activity for both features in auditory cortex and superior parietal lobule
location decodable from angular and supramarginal gyrus and both superior and inferior frontal gyrus
no region exclusively coded pitch memory information
interpretations: two features of a single auditory object are concurrently maintained with shared and feature-specific neural substrates
superior parietal lobule preferentially represents auditory spatial WM
